name: google-java-format-for-vs-code
displayName: Google Java Format for VS Code
description: Visual Studio Code extension to format Java files using google-java-format program to comply with Google Java Style.

configuration.title: Google Java Format for VS Code

java.format.settings.google.executable.markdownDescription: *(Override — not recommended.)* URL or local file path to a formatter executable. When set, `style`, `version`, and `mode` are all ignored.

java.format.settings.google.style.markdownDescription: Formatter variant to use. `palantir` is a lambda-friendly variant that requires `native-binary` mode and is unavailable on Windows and macOS x86-64.
markdownEnumDescriptions: [Google Java Format](https://github.com/google/google-java-format) — official Google Java style. Supports `native-binary` and `jar-file` modes.
[Palantir Java Format](https://github.com/palantir/palantir-java-format) — lambda-friendly variant. Supports `native-binary` only. Not available on Windows or macOS x86-64.

java.format.settings.google.version.markdownDescription: Formatter version to download and use. Accepts `latest` or a release tag (e.g. `1.25.2` for Google, `2.89.0` for Palantir). Ignored when `executable` is set.

java.format.settings.google.mode.markdownDescription: Runtime artifact to use. `jar-file` is only supported for `google` style and requires a compatible Java runtime on PATH. Ignored when `executable` is set.
markdownEnumDescriptions: Run the formatter as a JAR via the local Java runtime. **Only available for `google` style.** Requires Java 21+ for google-java-format ≥ 1.22.0.
Run the platform-specific native binary (no JVM required). Falls back to `jar-file` when no native binary is available for the current platform (`google` style only).

java.format.settings.google.extra.markdownDescription: Extra CLI arguments to pass to the formatter (e.g. `--aosp` for Android Open Source Project style).

commands.title: Google Java Format For VS Code: Reload Executable
Google Java Format For VS Code: Clear Cache
